channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky', a gripping seven-part thriller about a con artist's double life
item.description: The article reviews the Apple TV+ series 'Lucky', starring Anya Taylor-Joy as a cunning con artist caught in a web of betrayal and danger. After stealing millions with her husband, Lucky discovers he ...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Advances with Legal Arguments and Witness Testimonies
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continues to unfold with significant developments. On July 15, 2026, the Senate impeachment court addressed multiple motions, including the subpoen...

item.title: Shiv Sena Corporator Gets Bail After Hospital Assault Allegations
item.description: Ramesh Mhatre, a Shiv Sena corporator from Maharashtra's Dombivli, was granted bail after being arrested for allegedly assaulting doctors and hospital staff. The incident occurred on July 6 when Mhatr...

item.title: The 13th Dalai Lama's 1910 Escape to India and His Return to Tibet
item.description: The article chronicles the historical journey of the 13th Dalai Lama, Tenzin Gyatso, who fled Tibet for India in 1910, years before his successor, the 14th Dalai Lama, made a similar escape in 1959. I...

item.title: Rosemary Odinga's Snail Farming Venture Generates Millions in Kenya
item.description: Rosemary Odinga, daughter of late former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, has built a successful snail farming business in Kenya, earning millions annually. The article details her journey into helicultur...

item.title: Calgary Dog Park Faces Safety Concerns Over Toxic Ohio Buckeye Trees
item.description: The Ohio Buckeye tree, planted by the City of Calgary in the Cambrian Heights dog park, has raised concerns among residents about pet safety due to its toxic nuts. These nuts can cause vomiting, diarr...

item.title: Cape Town Approves New Data Centres Amid Environmental Concerns
item.description: Two massive data centres, to be built next to Cape Town International Airport, have received initial regulatory approval from the Municipal Planning Tribunal. The project, led by Equinix, involves con...

item.title: Former Police Minister Bheki Cele Testifies at TRC Inquiry Regarding Anti-Apartheid Activist's Disappearance
item.description: Former police minister Bheki Cele expressed deep distress during his testimony at the Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) inquiry, responding to allegations that he withheld information about th...

item.title: EU Funds Sub-Saharan African Universities with 4 Billion Shillings
item.description: The European Union has allocated Sh4 billion to strengthen higher education institutions across Sub-Saharan Africa, aiming to address infrastructure gaps, research capabilities, and academic resources...

item.title: Private hospital in Canberra's inner south to close due to financial strain
item.description: A private hospital in Canberra's inner south is set to close due to financial pressures, marking another setback for the private healthcare sector. Nexus Hospitals, the operator of the facility, has c...

item.title: US-Iran Tensions Lead to Hormuz Closure Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for oil and gas shipments from the Gulf, remains closed as Iran's Revolutionary Guards demand the US end its 'acts of aggression'. The conflict, which began in late...

item.title: Toronto's population decline: A crisis of urban governance and affordability
item.description: A recent analysis by the Toronto Metropolitan University reveals a significant exodus from Toronto, with residents citing systemic issues such as inadequate public transit, unaffordable housing, risin...

item.title: IBM Faces Financial Challenges Amid Profit Warnings
item.description: IBM, a leading technology company, recently issued a profit warning that has raised concerns among investors and analysts. The warning, which highlights declining revenues and increased operational co...

item.title: Three Suspects Arrested in Doctor's Robbery and Murder Case in Karachi
item.description: Karachi police have arrested three suspects allegedly involved in the fatal shooting of a young doctor during a robbery near Teen Talwar in Clifton. The 28-year-old doctor was killed during a robbery ...

item.title: Verification of Mitch McConnell's 'Proof of Life' Photo and Health Rumors
item.description: The article discusses the verification of Senator Mitch McConnell's 'proof of life' photo, which sparked conspiracy theories about his health. An intern at the Washington Post investigated the image, ...

item.title: AI Tools Transform Farming Practices in Canada Through Data-Driven Insights
item.description: Canadian agtech startup CropMind is leveraging artificial intelligence to revolutionize farming practices, helping growers make data-driven decisions and reduce uncertainty. The cloud-based SaaS platf...

item.title: Camila Alves: A Look at Her Career as a Model and Entrepreneur
item.description: Camila Alves, wife of actor Matthew McConaughey, is a Brazilian-American entrepreneur, model, designer, author, and philanthropist. Born in Brazil, she moved to the U.S. at 15, working odd jobs before...

item.title: Edmonton Councillor Michael Janz Faces Concerns Over Social Media Posts on Gaza Conflict
item.description: Edmonton city councillor Michael Janz has faced criticism from the Jewish Federation of Edmonton for sharing pro-Palestine content on his Instagram stories, which some argue spreads misinformation abo...

item.title: Tanzania's Tourism Sector Surpasses $4.41 Billion in 2025, Driven by Increased Visitor Spending
item.description: Tanzania's tourism sector reached a historic milestone in 2025, generating $4.41 billion in revenue, driven by higher visitor spending that outpaced growth in international arrivals. The country's tou...

item.title: Lagos Launches Tree Planting Initiative to Combat Climate Change and Urbanization
item.description: The Lagos State Governor, Babatunde Sanwo-Olu, has launched a comprehensive tree planting initiative aimed at addressing climate change, urbanization challenges, and environmental degradation. The pro...
